rows.llm_rationale: The crowd price of 7% appears too low given current macro conditions. As of April 2026, with inflation moderating and potential economic slowdown risks, 3 cuts by year-end (8 months remaining) implies roughly one cut every 2.7 months—plausible under stress scenarios but not baseline. CME FedWatch typically shows 15-20% probability for 3+ cuts in this timeframe under normal conditions; the 7% suggests market is pricing high conviction against cuts, likely overweighting near-term rate hold expectations.
rows.question: Will 3 Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?

rows.llm_rationale: Ethereum flipping (ETH > BTC market cap) requires ~3.3x ETH appreciation vs BTC or vice versa in 8 months. Historical flips are rare; ETH has never exceeded BTC in market cap despite multiple bull cycles. Crowd price of 42% appears slightly optimistic given structural barriers (BTC's network effects, store-of-value narrative, institutional adoption) and base rate of ~2% annually. Modest volume and tight liquidity suggest uncertain market expectations.
rows.question: Ethereum flipped in 2026?
